body: How I shoot, light, and color my work. Warm natural light, earth tones, textured surfaces. Editorial, restaurant, brand, and cookbook food photography out of Williamsburg, Brooklyn.
title: About Jesse Page Photography

body: Booking a shoot. Editorial day rate $1,800–$2,200. Restaurant half-day $1,200–$1,500, standard 25-shot deliverable. Brand work $500–$3,000 per deliverable. Email jesse.page@jessepagephotography.com to check availability.
title: Rates & Booking Guide

body: What to expect on a restaurant shoot day. Pre-light, menu walkthrough with the chef, tabletop and plating passes, tethered capture in Capture One. Delivery via Lightroom-edited gallery within 5 business days.
title: Restaurant Shoot Day: What to Expect

body: Step-by-step: ordering a print from the storefront. Choose size, finish, and frame, then check out through Etsy or Amazon. Prints ship FedEx, insured over $1,000.
title: How to Order a Fine-Art Print

body: Licensing food images for editorial and commercial use. Per-image and campaign licenses available. Usage, term, and territory set per agreement. Contact my agent Tomás Herrera at Herrera Creative Management.
title: Image Licensing & Usage Terms

body: My kit. Sony A7IV, Sony 90mm macro, Sony 24–70mm f/2.8, two Profoto B10 lights. Edited in Adobe Lightroom and Photoshop, tethered in Capture One on a MacBook Pro 16" M3.
title: Gear & Setup

body: Prepping your space for a brand shoot. South-facing natural light preferred. Clear surfaces, neutral linens, and any hero props ready. I bring backdrops, modifiers, and styling kit.
title: Preparing for a Brand Shoot

body: FAQ: turnaround, revisions, travel, and dietary styling. Note: I style dairy-free wherever possible. Travel within NYC included; outside metro billed separately.
title: Frequently Asked Questions
